title: East Mosul — The Offensive Begins
introText:
October 2016. The offensive opened yesterday.
You take a freelancer in on the second day. American. First war.
A consumer DSLR. A MacBook. No satellite phone. Two hundred dollars in cash.
Engine on at 05:10. Press plates outnumber everything else on the road south.

beats.text:
Four car bombs hit the convoy this morning, before you were even on the road.
Traffic control cleared the route by nine.

endings.text:
You drop him back in Erbil after dark. He doesn't know yet if any of it sells.
A piece runs in Harper's six weeks later — six hundred words, his byline, buried in the back of the issue.

The footage doesn't sell. Every desk already has convoy video from the first day.
He flies home in December. The DSLR goes back to a pawn shop in Ohio before New Year's.
